explanation: The clause describes a discrete event of coming after Jesus, rather than a situation holding continuously across a time span.
The verb 'has given up' entails a transition into a new state, and the focus is on that transition rather than on a situation holding over time.
The clause describes a discrete event (giving work) rather than a situation holding continuously across a time span.
The clause describes a discrete bounded event ('have done only one hour's work') rather than a situation holding continuously across the time span.
The clause describes a completed action of making them equal, rather than a situation holding continuously across a time span.

The clause 'if the master of the house had known what time the thief was coming' describes a state ('known') that would have held continuously from the time the thief was coming until the reference time, thus instantiating DtUniv.

The clause describes a single event (vandalism) that would not have occurred, rather than a situation holding continuously across a time span.
